# Notation and Definitions {#sec-ch02-notation}

> _"Mathematics is the language in which God has written the universe."_
>
> — Galileo Galilei

This chapter is a compact reference for the core math used at the start of the book.
It focuses on notation, definitions, and small numerical examples.
Later chapters introduce the additional math they need at the point where it becomes useful.

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/vectors.qmd >}}

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/dot-product.qmd >}}

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/matrix-multiply.qmd >}}

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/softmax.qmd >}}

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/logarithm.qmd >}}

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/mean-variance.qmd >}}

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/cross-entropy.qmd >}}

{{< include book/02-notation/sections/takeaways.qmd >}}
